title: Pilosella melaneilema
taxonomy: Pilosella melaneilema is a species of plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Pilosella, which is part of the larger tribe Senecioneae. The genus Pilosella itself is closely related to the genus Senecio, with some sources considering them to be a single genus. Pilosella melaneilema is also sometimes referred to as a hybrid or a complex species due to its uncertain taxonomic classification. Further research and classification may be needed to fully understand its place within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Pilosella melaneilema, also known as the black-leaved hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella melaneilema include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilosella melaneilema is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and wounds.
